name: Base Building
description: Players place, upgrade, persist, and defend structures that provide storage, shelter, crafting, or territory control.
design_notes: Base building touches placement validation, structural support, ownership, persistence, navmesh changes, and multiplayer permissions. Every base_structure_id should have rules for snapping, collision, terrain fit, support loss, repair, decay, and how shelter volumes affect survival systems.
design_purpose: Give players a long-term home, storage hub, crafting center, and recovery space.
Turn gathered resources into visible world progress and strategic protection.
Create player-authored layouts that interact with weather, enemies, crafting, and multiplayer ownership.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can carve out a safe place in a hostile world and improve it over time.

parameters.name: base_structure_id
parameters.description: Stable structure identifier used for placement, persistence, repair, ownership, and replication.

parameters.name: placement_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule set that validates terrain, collision, slope, sockets, permissions, and blocked zones.

parameters.name: structural_support_id
parameters.description: Support graph node or rule that decides whether a structure remains stable.

parameters.name: snap_socket_id
parameters.description: Optional socket used to align new pieces to existing structures.

parameters.name: ownership_permission_id
parameters.description: Permission rule for building, editing, opening containers, repairing, or demolishing structures.

parameters.name: shelter_volume_id
parameters.description: Generated or authored volume that tells temperature, weather, and safety systems the player is sheltered.

edge_cases: Destroying a foundation removes support for child structures that contain inventories, queues, or sleeping players.
A player places a structure while terrain, navmesh, or another player-built piece changes underneath it.
A shelter volume is partially enclosed and systems disagree whether rain, cold, enemies, or spawns are blocked.
A server restarts after placement resources were consumed but before the structure persistence record was committed.
common_bugs: Placement preview accepts a location but server validation rejects it due to different collision or slope checks.
Structures persist visually but ownership, inventory links, or crafting station queues are lost after load.
Navmesh and AI spawn blockers are not rebuilt after base edits, letting enemies spawn inside sealed rooms.
Support graph updates cascade in nondeterministic order and delete valid connected structures.
balancing_notes: Base safety should reward planning without removing all survival pressure from weather, raids, or resource needs.
Upkeep and decay can control server clutter, but opaque decay feels punishing when players return after a break.
Placement restrictions should prevent exploits while still allowing creative layouts.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear placement previews with text reasons for blocked placement, support failure, or permission denial.
Support keyboard and controller placement nudging, rotation steps, and snap toggles for precise building.

implementation_notes.unity: Use a placement service that tests collision, slope, sockets, ownership, and structural_support_id before consuming materials. Commit base_structure_id, transform, owner, support links, shelter volume membership, and station or container references in one save transaction.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent structures as data-backed nodes with stable ids and placement rules stored in Resources. Rebuild collision, navigation regions, and shelter volumes after accepted edits, and persist support graph edges before spawning cosmetic construction effects.
implementation_notes.unreal: Server-authoritative building actors should validate placement using collision queries, sockets, gameplay tags, and ownership permissions. Replicate accepted structure ids and transforms, then update navmesh, shelter, and interaction components on clients.
implementation_notes.web: Keep base pieces in a spatial index keyed by base_structure_id. For Canvas or Three.js builders, separate preview rendering from authoritative placement and store support links plus ownership in compact save data.

example_games.title: Valheim
example_games.note: Uses structural support, crafting stations, and shelter as base-building concerns.

example_games.title: Rust
example_games.note: Uses player bases for storage, ownership, defense, and multiplayer conflict.
